summary: Wave 2 verification: VIP Roulette + Baccarat backend endpoints, VipCrownBadge global UI, and standard-engine isolation. Created /app/backend/tests/test_high_roller_wave2_roulette_baccarat.py (14 tests, ALL PASS). Regression shield re-run: 349/349 GREEN. Frontend Playwright sweep verified: non-VIP demo user sees high-roller-not-vip + tier grid (no lounge tiles); after granting VIP via apply_vip_grant the same demo user sees all 3 lounge tiles (blackjack/roulette/baccarat), the wheel UI with 9 bet buttons + spin button, the baccarat player/banker/tie selectors + deal button, and the gold VipCrownBadge floats on /dashboard while correctly self-hiding on every /casino/high-roller* page. Isolation guarantee re-verified: standard /api/roulette/spin still returns 200 with NO 10k floor, and /api/baccarat/play never leaks 'high_roller' or '10000' into its response.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/high_roller.py grew from ~307 → 492 lines but remains cohesive; logical grouping into 4 sections (Models / Helpers / Endpoints / Grant) is clean. No need to split yet (under 700-line guideline).
Roulette payout uses int(b.bet_amount * (multiplier+1)) for the hit branch — correct (stake refunded + N:1 winnings). Loss branch returns 0 — correct.
Baccarat banker payout uses int(req.bet_amount * 1.95) — confirmed: 10000 → 19500 in live test (5% commission applied). Tie returns 9x and player returns 2x as documented.
VipCrownBadge self-hides on /casino/high-roller* via location.pathname.startsWith — verified live for both /dashboard (visible) and /casino/high-roller/* (hidden).

vip_roulette_server_hash takes user_id as a raw query/body param (FastAPI converts to query param). Test uses `params={'user_id': ...}` and gets 200; if a client sent it as JSON body the route would 422. Minor — consider a Pydantic body model for symmetry with other VIP routes. Non-blocking.
_RED_NUMBERS set matches the standard European wheel (18 entries) — Vegas-accurate.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (VIP & non-VIP flows verified; all 14 expected data-testids present)

test_credentials: Demo login button (login-demo-btn) — produces stable demo user_id 'demo_23970ac9'. Granted/revoked VIP via apply_vip_grant during the run.
seed_data_creation: Test fixture creates TEST_vip_w2_* users in high_roller_vip with 30-day vip_until, cleaned up in fixture teardown. Demo user VIP (granted for the frontend UI test) was also cleaned up post-run via pymongo delete_one.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `cd /app/backend && set -a && source .env && set +a && DISABLE_BG_SCHEDULERS=1 pytest tests/test_high_roller_wave2_roulette_baccarat.py tests/test_high_roller_mvp.py tests/regression_shield.py -q` — expect 14+14+349=377 pass. To re-test the frontend VIP path, grant VIP to the demo user with: `python3 -c "import asyncio; from routes.high_roller import apply_vip_grant; asyncio.run(apply_vip_grant(user_id='demo_23970ac9', tier='genesis', stripe_session_id='cs_test_X'))"` then login via the demo button and visit /casino/high-roller. The badge polls every 60s but mounts immediately on page load, so navigation between routes is sufficient. Clean up afterwards with `db.high_roller_vip.delete_one({'user_id':'demo_23970ac9'})`.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no issues found. All Wave 2 acceptance criteria pass on first run.
